params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Bandwidth account

params.name: messageId
params.description: Filter by a specific message ID

params.name: sourceTn
params.description: Filter by source telephone number in E.164 format

params.name: destinationTn
params.description: Filter by destination telephone number in E.164 format

params.name: messageStatus
params.description: Filter by message delivery status

params.name: messageDirection
params.description: Filter by message direction

params.name: carrierName
params.description: Filter by carrier name

params.name: messageType
params.description: Filter by message type

params.name: errorCode
params.description: Filter by error code

params.name: fromDateTime
params.description: Filter messages sent on or after this date-time

params.name: toDateTime
params.description: Filter messages sent on or before this date-time

params.name: pageToken
params.description: Page token for pagination

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of messages to return

items.docs: Retrieves a list of message metadata for the account. Bandwidth does not store message content for privacy reasons; only metadata such as sender, recipient, timestamps, and delivery status is returned. Supports filtering by source number, destination number, and date range.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Bandwidth account

items.docs: Sends an SMS or MMS message from a Bandwidth number. For MMS, include media URLs in the request body. Bandwidth will return an HTTP 202 Accepted indicating the message has been queued for delivery. Delivery status will be provided via webhook callbacks.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Bandwidth account

params.name: continuationToken
params.description: Token for paginating through large media lists

items.docs: Retrieves a list of all media files that have been uploaded to the account. Each request returns a maximum of 1000 media files. Bandwidth retains uploaded media for up to 48 hours.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Bandwidth account

params.name: mediaName
params.description: The name of the media file to download

items.docs: Downloads a specific media file by its name. Returns the binary content of the media file.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Bandwidth account

params.name: mediaName
params.description: The filename to assign to the uploaded media

items.docs: Uploads a media file for use in MMS messages. Files can be up to 3.75 MB in size. Bandwidth provides free file storage for an unlimited number of files with 48-hour retention.

params.name: accountId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Bandwidth account

params.name: mediaName
params.description: The name of the media file to delete

items.docs: Permanently deletes a specific media file from the account.
